w:r.w:t: n8n Email Outreach + CRM Automation - Technical Research Brief
A complete lead-to-reply pipeline in n8n:
Excel/CSV Leads → Import to Google Sheets → Email Validation →
AI Categorization → Smart Scoring → Daily Shortlist →
Personalized Outreach via Gmail → Follow-up Sequences →
Reply Detection → Status Update → CRM Dashboard
This document covers every component choice, its pros and cons, pricing, and the recommended architecture.
1. CRM Data Store: Where Leads Live
Handles millions of rows without slowing down
ACID compliant and no data loss or corruption
Requires hosting ($20-40/month VPS) + dashboard for non-technical team
Zero cost, team can view/edit immediately
Easy to accidentally break or delete data
It matches the BRD spec, the team is familiar with it, it costs nothing, and for a lead list under 5,000 rows it works fine.
if lead volume exceeds 5,000 and n8n workflows can swap data sources without rebuilding the logic.
2. Email Verification: Don't Send to Bad Addresses
- it has the most mature n8n integration (officially verified node), includes AI scoring which feeds directly into the lead scoring workflow (WF-CRM-3), and pricing is reasonable for their volume.
3. Email Sending: How to Actually Send
This is the most critical decision. Get it wrong and emails land in spam.
n8n connects to Gmail via OAuth. Sends emails as the authenticated user. Native n8n node available.
● Free Gmail: 500 emails/day, but only ~100 via SMTP/API
● Google Workspace: 2,000 emails/day per account officially
Cold email safe limit: ~25–50 emails per inbox per day
as this is the practical number before spam filters trigger, regardless of what the official limit says
● Emails come from a real Gmail/Workspace address (better deliverability than bulk senders for small volumes)
● Replies land in the same inbox naturally
● Zero additional cost if Workspace already exists
● Native n8n Gmail node handles send + read + labels
● Reply tracking is built into Gmail (just poll for responses)
● Google actively monitors for cold email behavior and can suspend the account
● No inbox rotation or warmup built in
● Sending identical messages to 200+ people gets flagged
● Account suspension risk if caught sending cold emails at volume

Use multiple Gmail accounts (3–5 Workspace accounts), rotate between them in n8n using a round-robin approach. At 25–50 emails per account per day, 5 accounts give you 125–250 sends/day. This is enough for EITACIES's current scale.
Dedicated email delivery service. n8n has a native SendGrid node. Emails are sent through SendGrid's infrastructure, but can be configured to appear from your domain.
● Built for volume - no account suspension risk
● Open and click tracking built in
● Domain authentication (SPF, DKIM, DMARC) guided setup
● Emails don't come from actual Gmail inbox - slightly different "feel"
● Replies need to be routed back (can set reply-to as Gmail address)
● Cold email is technically against SendGrid TOS if recipients haven't opted in
● High deliverability if domain is properly warmed
● Complex setup (AWS SigV4 auth, no native n8n node — requires HTTP Request node)
● Need to manage bounces and complaints manually
● No open/click tracking without extra setup
4. AI Categorization & Scoring: GPT-4o vs Alternatives
GPT-4o: ~10/1M output tokens. For lead scoring, each call is ~500 tokens in + 200 tokens out ≈ $0.003 per lead.
- Hiring signals: {yes/no}, {number of active job postings}
Classify this lead as: Hot, Warm, Cold, or Not Relevant.
Gemini 1.5 Flash is cheaper than GPT-4o for simple classification tasks.

Use rule-based scoring for the 0–100 lead score (it's deterministic and doesn't need AI). Use GPT-4o for the Hot/Warm/Cold/Not Relevant classification where the AI can weigh multiple fuzzy signals together.
This keeps costs near-zero for scoring while using AI where it genuinely adds value.
5. Email Outreach Sequences: How Follow-ups Work
n8n doesn't have a built-in "sequence" feature like Instantly or Mailshake. You build it yourself using scheduled triggers, status tracking, and conditional logic.
→ If stage = 0: Send initial email, set stage = 1, set last_contacted = today
→ If stage = 1 AND days_since_contact >= 3 AND no reply: Send follow-up 1, set stage = 2
→ If stage = 2 AND days_since_contact >= 5 AND no reply: Send follow-up 2, set stage = 3
→ Gmail node: Search for replies in inbox
→ Match reply sender email to lead in Sheet
Use the OpenAI node to generate personalized email bodies using lead data. Or use template variables from the Sheet:
Subject: Quick question about {company_name}'s hiring plans
Body: Hi {first_name}, I noticed {company_name} is growing -
7. n8n Workflow Breakdown - What Gets Built
n8n instance setup, Google Sheets CRM structure, Gmail OAuth connections, domain auth (SPF/DKIM/DMARC)
WF-2: Apollo enrichment + Apify hiring signals + AI categorization
WF-3: Scoring rules + WF-4: Daily shortlist
WF-5: Full outreach sequence with personalization, follow-ups, opt-out
, testing, warmup initiation, edge case handling
A Google Sheet with a summary tab using formulas/charts:
Full CRM dashboard beyond Google Sheets (would need a proper frontend)
